_note: RQ4 honesty gap: user-concern prevalence (coded reviews) x trace measurability (Pillar A/B). base = reviews with >=1 substantive (non-other) code.

rows.why: "is my loved one safe / where expected" needs reliable reachability/place/being-with-people — Pillar B r=0.10-0.34, Pillar A labeling flat/degrades
battery/crash/perf = systems-quality concern, orthogonal to presence-construct validity
who-can-see / hide / per-contact = UX/policy controls, not a construct the trace must infer
staleness = exact (last-fix timestamp); the system CAN honestly know and show it
geometric proximity/ETA is exact, but "arrived / en route / reachable" social inference is weak (K4)
surveillance/coercion = policy/UX/ethics concern; no trace inference satisfies it
positional error is quantifiable; only the K1 role-labeling is weak
a privacy/utility design choice (how coarse to show), not an inference-validity question
spoof/pause/"is it real" = integrity/provenance — companion (ZKLS) territory, cited not claimed
